title: chroot, run: drop Pdeathsig
body:
it is not safe to use prctl(PR_SET_PDEATHSIG) in a Go program since the parent of the process is considered the *thread* that created the
child process, and the Go runtime can free a thread at any time.   We
would either need to lock the thread, or simpler just not use it.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17918

Doug Rabson @dfr added this for his BSD purposes.  I'd like him to chime in on this too before we merge.
I mainly added the FreeBSD pdeathsig handling for consistency with Linux. If this is wrong for Linux, then its almost certainly also wrong for FreeBSD.

LGTM
but definitely want @nalind  to scope it out, plus some of the tests aren't hip.
So if I'm using chroot isolation to handle a RUN instruction or the like, and buildah gets a ctrl-c, what happens to the child processes after this change?

> So if I'm using chroot isolation to handle a RUN instruction or the like, and buildah gets a ctrl-c

Or the uncatchable SIGKILL, I suppose.

> So if I'm using chroot isolation to handle a RUN instruction or the like, and buildah gets a ctrl-c, what happens to the child processes after this change?

Good point.  I've restored it for the `chroot` package and added a new patch to make sure the current thread is locked (so that it won't be released while we wait for the command).
@nalind are you fine with the last version?

It should be fine. When we use an external runtime, we don't directly create the processes that we intend should be killed off if we are, and there's no runtime config field that would let us specify that, so this is probably as good as we can expect.
